pattern: Greedy insight on chained division: dividing the first number by the parenthesized division of the rest maximizes the result, since every term after the first lands in the denominator's denominator and thus the numerator.
hints: Think about which positions in a left-to-right chain of divisions can be pushed into the numerator versus the denominator.
The first value is always the overall numerator; you want every remaining value (except the second) to multiply the result, which happens when they sit inside a nested denominator.
With three or more numbers the answer is the first number divided by a single parenthesized group containing all the rest; with one or two numbers no parentheses help.

edge_inputs.description: longer chain still groups everything after the first

stress.description: random valid chains within value bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    arr = [rng.randint(2, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [arr]
stress.note: string output is fully determined by length
